howItWorks.intro: The plate loading calculator determines exactly which plates to put on each side of the barbell to reach your target weight.
rules: Enter your target total weight (bar + plates)
The calculator subtracts the bar weight, divides by 2 for each side
It uses a greedy algorithm starting with the largest available plate to find the optimal combination
If the exact weight can't be matched, it shows the closest possible combination
howItWorks.outro: This works with any bar weight and any combination of available plates. Standard Olympic plates are pre-selected but you can customize which plates you have available.

faq.question: How much does an Olympic barbell weigh?
faq.answer: A standard Olympic barbell weighs 45 lbs (20 kg). Women's Olympic bars weigh 33 lbs (15 kg). Some specialty bars like trap bars or safety squat bars may weigh differently.

faq.question: What plates are standard at most gyms?
faq.answer: Most gyms stock plates in these sizes: 45 lb, 35 lb, 25 lb, 10 lb, 5 lb, and 2.5 lb. Some gyms also have 1.25 lb micro plates for smaller increments.

faq.question: Why can't I hit my exact target weight?
faq.answer: Barbells can only be loaded in increments based on available plates. The smallest standard increment is 5 lbs (2.5 lb plate on each side). If your target falls between possible increments, the calculator shows the closest achievable weight.

faq.question: Does plate order on the bar matter?
faq.answer: For safety, load the heaviest plates closest to the collar and work outward to the lightest. This keeps the center of gravity stable and makes it easier to load and unload.

faq.question: How do I convert between lbs and kg plates?
faq.answer: Common conversions: 45 lb ≈ 20 kg, 25 lb ≈ 10 kg, 10 lb ≈ 5 kg, 5 lb ≈ 2.5 kg, 2.5 lb ≈ 1.25 kg. Most metric gyms use 20, 15, 10, 5, 2.5, and 1.25 kg plates.

howWeCalculate.intro: This plate loading calculator uses established formulas and industry-standard data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs
Results are calculated instantly in your browser — nothing is sent to a server
Review the detailed breakdown to understand how each factor affects your result
howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This plate loading calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable estimates without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.
scenarios: When you need a quick estimate before committing to a purchase or project
When comparing different options or scenarios side by side
When planning a budget and need to understand potential costs
When you want to verify a quote or estimate you've received from a professional
When teaching or learning about the concepts behind these calculations
